questions.question: Substance L melts at –7 °C and is a brown liquid at room temperature. What is the boiling point of pure L? A –77 °C, B –7 °C to +7 °C, C 59 °C, D 107 °C to 117 °C
questions.text: Substance L melts at –7 °C and is a brown liquid at room temperature. What is the boiling point of pure L? A –77 °C, B –7 °C to +7 °C, C 59 °C, D 107 °C to 117 °C

questions.question: Which row describes how the volume of a gas changes when its temperature is increased at constant pressure and when its pressure is increased at constant temperature? temperature is increased at constant pressure pressure is increased at constant temperature A volume decreases volume decreases, B volume decreases volume increases, C volume increases volume decreases, D volume increases volume increases
questions.text: Which row describes how the volume of a gas changes when its temperature is increased at constant pressure and when its pressure is increased at constant temperature? temperature is increased at constant pressure pressure is increased at constant temperature A volume decreases volume decreases, B volume decreases volume increases, C volume increases volume decreases, D volume increases volume increases

questions.question: Nitrogen dioxide is a red-brown gas which is more dense than air. The  diagram  shows  the  arrangement  of  two  gas  jars  which  contain  nitrogen  dioxide  and  air separated with a glass plate. colourless air glass plate red-brown nitrogen dioxide The glass plate is removed, and the gas jars are left for 24 hours. Which diagram shows the gas jars after 24 hours? ABDC 3
questions.text: Nitrogen dioxide is a red-brown gas which is more dense than air. The  diagram  shows  the  arrangement  of  two  gas  jars  which  contain  nitrogen  dioxide  and  air separated with a glass plate. colourless air glass plate red-brown nitrogen dioxide The glass plate is removed, and the gas jars are left for 24 hours. Which diagram shows the gas jars after 24 hours? ABDC 3

questions.question: Which substance is a mixture of one element and one compound? A aqueous glucose, B clean, dry air, C oxygen dissolved in distilled water, D stainless steel
questions.text: Which substance is a mixture of one element and one compound? A aqueous glucose, B clean, dry air, C oxygen dissolved in distilled water, D stainless steel
options: C oxygen dissolved in distilled water

questions.question: Which statement defines the atomic number of an element? A the group number of the element, B the mass of one atom of the element, C the number of particles in the nucleus of one atom of the element, D the number of protons in the nucleus of one atom of the element
questions.text: Which statement defines the atomic number of an element? A the group number of the element, B the mass of one atom of the element, C the number of particles in the nucleus of one atom of the element, D the number of protons in the nucleus of one atom of the element
options: A the group number of the element
B the mass of one atom of the element
C the number of particles in the nucleus of one atom of the element
D the number of protons in the nucleus of one atom of the element

questions.question: What is different for isotopes of the same element? A nucleon number, B number of electron shells, C number of electrons in the outer electron shell, D proton number
questions.text: What is different for isotopes of the same element? A nucleon number, B number of electron shells, C number of electrons in the outer electron shell, D proton number
options: C number of electrons in the outer electron shell

questions.question: When sodium chloride is formed from its elements, each chlorine atom ......1...... one ......2...... . Which words correctly complete gaps 1 and 2? 1   2 A gains    electron, B gains    proton, C loses    electron, D loses    proton

questions.text: In the molecules CH 4 , HCl and H 2 O, which atoms use all of their outer shell electrons in bonding? A C and Cl B C and H C Cl and H D H and O 4
options: A C and Cl B C and H C Cl and H D H and O 4

questions.question: The diagrams show the structures of four molecules. CH H H H C H HC HH CCH H H O H HC H C CC O H HH H HH CCC H H HH O HH C Which statement about these molecules is correct? A All four molecules have different molecular formulae., B All four molecules have the same molecular formula., C Only two molecules have the same molecular formula., D Only three molecules have the same molecular formula.
questions.text: The diagrams show the structures of four molecules. CH H H H C H HC HH CCH H H O H HC H C CC O H HH H HH CCC H H HH O HH C Which statement about these molecules is correct? A All four molecules have different molecular formulae., B All four molecules have the same molecular formula., C Only two molecules have the same molecular formula., D Only three molecules have the same molecular formula.
options: A All four molecules have different molecular formulae.
B All four molecules have the same molecular formula.
C Only two molecules have the same molecular formula.
D Only three molecules have the same molecular formula.

questions.question: The equation for the reaction of magnesium with oxygen is shown. 2Mg  +  O 2   2MgO Which mass of oxygen reacts exactly with 6 g of magnesium? A 2 g B 4 g C 8 g D 16 g 5
questions.text: The equation for the reaction of magnesium with oxygen is shown. 2Mg  +  O 2   2MgO Which mass of oxygen reacts exactly with 6 g of magnesium? A 2 g B 4 g C 8 g D 16 g 5
options: A 2 g B 4 g C 8 g D 16 g 5

questions.question: Which reactions are redox reactions? 1   the incomplete combustion of propane 2   the rusting of iron 3   the reaction of aqueous chloride ions with acidified aqueous silver nitrate 4   the thermal decomposition of calcium carbonate A 1 and 2 B  1 and 3 C  2 and 4 D  3 and 4
questions.text: Which reactions are redox reactions? 1   the incomplete combustion of propane 2   the rusting of iron 3   the reaction of aqueous chloride ions with acidified aqueous silver nitrate 4   the thermal decomposition of calcium carbonate A 1 and 2 B  1 and 3 C  2 and 4 D  3 and 4
options: A 1 and 2 B  1 and 3 C  2 and 4 D  3 and 4

questions.question: Which row describes an aqueous solution for the pH shown? pH description A 1 has a very high alkalinity, B 5 is neutral, C 9     turns universal indicator yellow, D 14 contains OH – ions 8
questions.text: Which row describes an aqueous solution for the pH shown? pH description A 1 has a very high alkalinity, B 5 is neutral, C 9     turns universal indicator yellow, D 14 contains OH – ions 8
options: A 1 has a very high alkalinity

questions.question: Which parts of the Periodic Table contain elements that only form basic oxides? A Group I and Period 3, B Group I only, C Group VIII and Period 3, D Group VIII only
questions.text: Which parts of the Periodic Table contain elements that only form basic oxides? A Group I and Period 3, B Group I only, C Group VIII and Period 3, D Group VIII only

questions.question: Which statement about elements in the Periodic Table is correct? A Elements in the same group of the Periodic Table have the same electronic configuration., B The atoms of the elements in Group VII form ions with a charge equal to the number of outer shell electrons minus 8., C The metallic character of the elements increases from left to right across a period., D The Periodic Table is an arrangement of elements in order of increasing nucleon number.
questions.text: Which statement about elements in the Periodic Table is correct? A Elements in the same group of the Periodic Table have the same electronic configuration., B The atoms of the elements in Group VII form ions with a charge equal to the number of outer shell electrons minus 8., C The metallic character of the elements increases from left to right across a period., D The Periodic Table is an arrangement of elements in order of increasing nucleon number.
options: A Elements in the same group of the Periodic Table have the same electronic configuration.
B The atoms of the elements in Group VII form ions with a charge equal to the number of outer shell electrons minus 8.
C The metallic character of the elements increases from left to right across a period.
D The Periodic Table is an arrangement of elements in order of increasing nucleon number.

questions.question: Which statements about the halogens are correct? 1   They are all diatomic molecules. 2   They are all gases at room temperature and pressure. 3   The density of the elements increases down the group. 4   The reactivity of the elements increases down the group. A 1 and 2 B  1 and 3 C  2 and 4 D  3 and 4
questions.text: Which statements about the halogens are correct? 1   They are all diatomic molecules. 2   They are all gases at room temperature and pressure. 3   The density of the elements increases down the group. 4   The reactivity of the elements increases down the group. A 1 and 2 B  1 and 3 C  2 and 4 D  3 and 4
options: A 1 and 2 B  1 and 3 C  2 and 4 D  3 and 4

questions.question: Which statements about metals are correct? 1   Calcium reacts faster than magnesium with cold water. 2   Aluminium is more easily extracted than iron from its ore. 3   Copper reacts faster than iron with dilute hydrochloric acid. 4   Potassium and silver are both good conductors of electricity. A 1 and 2 B  1 and 4 C  2 and 3 D 3 and 4
questions.text: Which statements about metals are correct? 1   Calcium reacts faster than magnesium with cold water. 2   Aluminium is more easily extracted than iron from its ore. 3   Copper reacts faster than iron with dilute hydrochloric acid. 4   Potassium and silver are both good conductors of electricity. questions.question: Pure metals can be combined to make alloys. Why are the alloys sometimes used rather than the metals they are made from? A Alloys are easier to extract and cheaper., B Alloys are easier to hammer into different shapes., C Alloys are harder and keep their shape better., D Alloys contain only atoms of the same size but pure metals do not.
questions.text: Pure metals can be combined to make alloys. Why are the alloys sometimes used rather than the metals they are made from? A Alloys are easier to extract and cheaper., B Alloys are easier to hammer into different shapes., C Alloys are harder and keep their shape better., D Alloys contain only atoms of the same size but pure metals do not.
options: A Alloys are easier to extract and cheaper.
B Alloys are easier to hammer into different shapes.
C Alloys are harder and keep their shape better.
D Alloys contain only atoms of the same size but pure metals do not.

B oxides of nitrogen and sulfur dioxide
